steps.title: Identify the claim's two components
steps.content: The historian claims carpa theater had slapstick comedy (exaggerated physical humor) that was shaped by audience reactions. A supporting quotation must illustrate both elements in El Teatro's style.

steps.title: Match both elements to the choices
steps.content: Choice A shows both: actos with “exaggerated physical humor” and audience reactions that “promoted improvisation.” This directly illustrates the slapstick-and-audience-responsive style of carpa theater. The other three address humor's social role, the troupe's aesthetic choices, or staging simplicity, but don't show this stylistic influence. That matches choice A — A is correct.
